slides.title: A little cold, a little *resilience*.
slides.sub: Brief cold exposure is a small, optional stressor some people lean into. Gentle and short is the whole idea.

slides.title: A small, deliberate stressor
slides.body: A brief moment of cold is a gentle challenge for the body. Small stresses *may help* build a little resilience.

slides.title: Alertness and mood
slides.body: Some people find a quick cold finish leaves them feeling awake and clear. The lift *can feel* refreshing.

slides.title: Start tiny
slides.body: Try a cold finish to your usual shower for ten or twenty seconds. There is *no need* to plunge.

slides.title: Keep it brief
slides.body: Short and comfortable beats long and forced. If it ever feels wrong, *simply stop*. This step is optional.

slides.title: A calm little practice
slides.body: Treat cold as a small, optional habit, not a test. For some people it *may add* a quiet spark.

slides.title: Small habits, *steadier days*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food and movement that help you age well, all in one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A brief cold finish to your shower is a small, optional habit some people enjoy. 🌿

Many report a lift in alertness and mood, and a little gentle stress may help build resilience. Start tiny and keep it short. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How a Little Cold Exposure May Support Resilience
